lastReleaseMessage: map beeswax to the honey illustration

barRaise.topFinding: The label engine stays healthy for the sixth run, and every open finding still lives in the commit-discipline and repo-hygiene perimeter, not the renderer. The one thing that changed since 2026-06-12 is that nothing changed: zero commits landed (last is 549ac53, v0.18.1, 2026-06-11), so the exact same nine-file diff that 06-12 flagged is byte-identical and still uncommitted, now across two full review boundaries. That diff is not throwaway work; it holds a QuotaExceededError surfacing feature in src/util/persist.js and the release.ps1 clean-tree guard that prevents the documented v0.17.1/v0.17.2 changelog double-fire. 'Commit and release the nine-file diff' was action 1 on both 06-11 and 06-12 and was not taken either time, so the reliability finding escalates to HIGH and zero actions closed this cycle. The fix is unchanged and is one release: ship the tree, cut v1.0, and drop YaA off the daily cadence so the same four findings stop re-printing against work nobody is acting on.
